semantic.role: AST-level language discovery utility that identifies, tracks, and resolves programming languages within the codebase. Provides snapshot and delta computation for language landscape tracking.
responsibilities: Scans a list of file paths and extracts unique file extensions to build a LanguageSnapshot.
Computes the difference (LanguageDelta) between two language snapshots, identifying new, removed, and status-changed extensions.
Resolves a file's programming language by delegating to LangRegistry based on its file extension.
Provides sorted output of detected extensions for consistent representation.
outOfScope: Does not perform actual code parsing, syntax analysis, or AST generation.
Does not manage language configuration or extension registration; that is handled by the LangRegistry module.
invariants: Each file extension maps to exactly one language via LangRegistry resolution.
The detected extensions in a snapshot are always sorted alphabetically.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Provide a language discovery layer that abstracts file extension-to-language mapping and tracks changes across scans, enabling the system to understand the language composition of the codebase.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Initial implementation of language discovery module as part of ArchSpine v1.0.0 architectural governance and semantic layer.

publicSurface.description: Scans an array of file paths, extracts unique file extensions, and returns a LanguageSnapshot containing the sorted list of detected extensions.
Compares two LanguageSnapshots and returns a LanguageDelta listing new, removed, and status-changed extensions between them.
Resolves the programming language for a given file path by delegating to LangRegistry, returning the language name and support level.

Resolves a file's programming language by delegating to LangRegistry based on its file extension.

outOfScope: Does not perform actual code parsing, syntax analysis, or AST generation.
Does not manage language configuration or extension registration; that is handled by the LangRegistry module.
